title: You Know Every Word Of These Songs — But Do You Know The Movie?
dek: One iconic musical moment, zero title given — can you name the movie behind each unforgettable number?

items.quote: Maria calms the terrified von Trapp kids during a thunderstorm by singing about the small, comforting things that make her happy.
Sandy debuts her leather-and-spandex glow-up at the carnival, and she and her summer fling finally admit they're stuck on each other in this closing duet.
A princess flees to an empty mountaintop and finally stops hiding her ice powers, deciding to embrace who she really is once and for all.
A struggling jazz pianist wanders a moonlit pier at night, quietly wondering if his big break — and the girl he's falling for — is actually within reach.
A troupe of sideshow performers finally stop apologizing for who they are and belt out the film's defiant, stand-tall anthem of self-acceptance.
A magical nanny convinces two skeptical kids that even the most boring chore turns fun the second you find the right way to look at it.
A penniless writer and a glamorous courtesan swear their love will survive absolutely anything the world throws at them, in this lush, operatic duet.
A farm girl in black-and-white Kansas dreams of a faraway, brightly colored place where all her troubles simply melt away.
